originalTitle: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
title: Big News from Our Neighbors, Chad and Sudan!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! Your friendly African news reporter is here, ready to share some important news from our continent. Today, we're going to talk about two countries that are neighbors, just like you might have neighbors next door to your house. These countries are called Chad and Sudan. They are both beautiful places in Africa, full of wonderful people and amazing animals! Sometimes, even good neighbors can have a misunderstanding or a problem. That's what we're going to talk about today, but don't worry, we'll explain everything in a simple way, and we'll always hope for peace and kindness.

What Happened?

Something very sad happened recently. In Chad, there were some people gathered together. These people were called "mourners." Mourners are people who are feeling very, very sad because someone they loved very much has passed away. They were all together, remembering their friend or family member. Now, imagine a special flying robot, like a toy airplane that flies very high and can be controlled by someone far away. We call these 'drones.' A drone from the country of Sudan accidentally flew over and caused a big problem where the sad mourners were. It was a big mistake, and it made many people very, very sad and hurt some people too. Seventeen people sadly lost their lives. This made everyone in Chad very worried and upset. Did you know? Drones can be used for many helpful things, like taking cool pictures from the sky, helping farmers check their fields, or even delivering important medicines to faraway places! But sometimes, like any machine, mistakes can happen.

Chad's President Says "Stop!"

When the sad news reached Chad's leader, President Idris Mahamat Déby, he was very upset and worried for his people. A President is like the main leader of a country. They help make important decisions and try to keep everyone safe. President Déby wants to make sure his people in Chad are safe and that nothing like this happens again. So, President Déby made some big decisions. First, he told everyone that the "border" between Chad and Sudan would be closed. Imagine a border like an invisible line on the ground or on a map. It shows where one country stops and another country begins. It's like how your yard has a fence, but for whole countries! Closing the border means it's harder for people and things to go from one country to the other for a little while. This is done to help keep everyone safe and prevent more problems. Second, President Déby told his "military" to be on "high alert." The military are like special helpers who work for the country. Their job is to keep everyone safe and protect the country from danger, like very brave guards. Being on "high alert" means they need to be extra, extra careful and ready for anything. Like when you're playing a game and you need to watch out for everything around you very carefully! Did you know? A President is like the head of a big family – the whole country! They work hard to make sure everyone in their country is happy and safe.

What is a Drone, Really?

Let's think a bit more about drones. A drone is like a small flying robot. Someone sits somewhere else, perhaps in a room with screens and joysticks, and flies the drone. It can see things from high up in the sky with its camera. Drones are used for so many good things, from filming movies to helping scientists study animals. But when they are used in a wrong way or by mistake, they can cause big problems, like what happened in Chad. Did you know? Some of the first ideas for flying machines that could be controlled from far away were thought of a very long time ago!

Why Are Borders Important?

Borders are super important for countries. They help countries know where their land is and who is in charge of what. Imagine drawing a line on the sand to show where your sandcastle begins and ends. That's a bit like a country's border! When a President closes a border, it's usually because they want to keep their people safe from something outside their country, or they want to make sure everyone is following the rules. It's like putting up a temporary fence to protect your home. Did you know? Some borders between countries are not just invisible lines; they can be big rivers, tall mountains, or even big lakes!

Being Good Neighbors

Chad and Sudan are next-door neighbors in Africa. Just like you and your friends, it's always best when neighbors can be friends and help each other. When neighbors have problems, it can make everyone feel sad and worried. It's like when two friends disagree during playtime; it's much better to talk about it and find a way to be friends again. Everyone in Chad wants peace. They want to be able to live safely and without worrying about unexpected things like a drone making a mistake. The President is trying to make sure that everyone in Chad is protected.

Hoping for Peace

Even when sad things happen, we always hope for peace. It's important for countries to talk to each other when there are problems, just like you would talk to a friend if you had a disagreement. Talking kindly and trying to understand each other is the best way to make things better. We all hope that Chad and Sudan can work together, talk about what happened, and find a way to be good neighbors again. Because in Africa, and all over the world, being kind and peaceful is the best way to live. Your friendly reporter will keep you updated! Stay kind, everyone!
summary: Chad's President closed the border with Sudan and put the military on high alert after a drone from Sudan accidentally hit people mourning in Chad, sadly killing 17 people, causing great sadness and worry.
whyMatters: This is important because Chad and Sudan are neighbors, and when neighbors have problems, it can make everyone in those countries feel worried and unsafe; it's always best for countries to talk and be peaceful friends.

originalTitle: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
title: A Big Football Surprise! What Happened to AFCON 2025?
content:
Hello, Little Sports Fans!

Hi everyone! Do you love playing football? Kicking the ball, running fast, and scoring goals? It's one of the most exciting games in the whole world, right? We all love watching our favourite teams play and cheer very loudly for them! Here in Africa, football is super, super special. It brings us all together, like one big family. Today, we have a little bit of news about a very important football event called AFCON, and a big surprise that happened.

What is AFCON? A Big Football Party!

Let's talk about AFCON first! AFCON stands for the Africa Cup of Nations. It's like the biggest, most exciting football party for all of Africa! Imagine all the best football teams from different African countries coming together to play amazing games. They play to see who is the champion team of Africa! People from all over Africa watch these games. They wear their team's special colours, sing songs, and wave flags. It's a time filled with so much joy and excitement for everyone. *Did you know?* The AFCON tournament happens every two years! That means we don't have to wait too long to see our favourite teams play again and again. How cool is that?

Senegal Was Going to Be the Host!

Now, for AFCON 2025, a wonderful country called Senegal was chosen to be the special host! What does "host" mean? It means Senegal was going to be the country where all the football games would happen. They would welcome all the teams and all the fans. Imagine you are having a birthday party, and you invite all your friends to your house. You are the host! Senegal was going to be like the host of this giant football birthday party for Africa. Everyone was very happy for them. Senegal is a beautiful country in West Africa, known for its friendly people and its amazing football players! They have a very strong national team, sometimes called the 'Lions of Teranga'. They were so excited to show everyone their lovely country.

Oh No! A Big Change!

But then, something surprising happened. A group of grown-ups who help organize African football made a new decision. They said that Senegal will *not* be the host for AFCON 2025 anymore. Yes, that's right, the plans changed. It's like planning a super fun picnic, and then the weather changes, and you can't have it outside anymore. Sometimes, big plans change. This news made many people feel a bit sad and confused, especially in Senegal and among football fans who were looking forward to it. This decision means that another country will now need to be chosen to host the big AFCON football games in 2025. It's a really big change for everyone involved in African football. *Did you know?* Hosting a big event like AFCON means building special stadiums and hotels, and getting everything ready for thousands of visitors! It's a huge job!

Why Hosting is Super Cool!

So, why is being the host country so super cool? Well, when a country hosts AFCON, lots of wonderful things happen! Many people come to visit from all over Africa and even the world. They stay in hotels, eat yummy food, and buy souvenirs. This helps the country's shops and businesses. It also helps people learn more about the host country, its culture, and its kind people. It's a chance for the host country to shine brightly on the world stage! Plus, it makes everyone in the host country feel very proud! Imagine your country welcoming all the other countries for such an important event. It's like everyone is cheering for your home, not just your team.

Meet Caf: The Grown-Ups of African Football

Who made this big decision? It was the CAF. CAF stands for the Confederation of African Football. They are the grown-ups who look after football rules and tournaments all over Africa. They make sure everything runs smoothly. CAF is like the big teacher or the principal for all the African football teams. They decide where the games will be played, when they will happen, and who plays whom. They have a very important job to make sure football in Africa is fair and fun for everyone. *Did you know?* CAF has its main office in Egypt! That's a country far away in North Africa. From there, they help organize all the big football events for the whole continent!

Mister Augustin Senghor is Unhappy

Now, a very important person named Mister Augustin Senghor (you can say "Ah-goo-stan Sen-gore") is a part of the CAF team. He works very hard for African football. He heard about the decision to take away Senegal's turn to host, and he was very unhappy. Mister Senghor said this decision was "unacceptable" and "abject". These are big words, but they just mean he thinks it's not okay and not fair. He believes it was a bad decision and that people should speak up about it. When someone says something is "unacceptable," it means they don't think it's right. When they "denounce" something, it means they are speaking out loudly to say it's wrong. Mister Senghor wants everyone to know he disagrees with this big change.

What Happens When Plans Change?

It can be disappointing when plans change, especially big, exciting plans like hosting AFCON. But even when things don't go the way we hoped, we can still love football and support our teams. Now, CAF will have to find a new country to host the AFCON 2025 tournament. It will be another African country that will get the chance to welcome everyone. We hope they will be ready to make it a fantastic football party! It's important to remember that even with changes, the spirit of football stays alive. It's about playing together, having fun, and cheering for your friends and your country. Even if your country isn't hosting, you can still enjoy the games!

Football Fun Never Stops!

So, while there might be some sadness about Senegal not hosting, the game of football itself continues to be wonderful. We will still see amazing goals, fantastic saves, and thrilling matches when AFCON 2025 finally arrives. We must remember to be good sports, even when we are disappointed. Everyone in Africa loves football, and we will all come together to celebrate our beautiful game, no matter where it is played. Let's keep cheering for our favourite teams and look forward to all the exciting football still to come! Maybe Senegal will host AFCON another time in the future. We can always hope! *Did you know?* The ball used in football is called a 'sphere'! It's round so it can roll and be kicked easily. Imagine trying to play with a square ball! Ha ha!

Did You Know? More Fun Football Facts!

* A football game lasts for 90 minutes, but it's split into two halves of 45 minutes each. There's a short break in the middle! * The biggest football stadium in Africa is called FNB Stadium in South Africa. It can hold almost 95,000 people! That's a LOT of fans! * Football is the most popular sport in the world! More people watch and play football than any other sport. * You don't just use your feet in football! Goalies can use their hands, and sometimes players use their heads to hit the ball! But never, ever their hands (unless they are the goalie inside their special box!). Keep loving football, everyone! It's a game of skill, teamwork, and big, big fun!
summary: Senegal will no longer host the big AFCON 2025 football games, making an important football leader, Mister Augustin Senghor, very unhappy because he believes it is not fair.
whyMatters: This matters because hosting AFCON is a special honor for a country, bringing joy, visitors, and showing off their beautiful nation, so changing the host makes many people sad and disappointed.
